violations.text: At the time of inspection, the establishment did not have a person certified in food safety on duty as required when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Owner to ensure extra individual(s) is certified in food safety to cover times when the Certified Food Safety Manager is not available. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

Previously prepared taco meat, hardboiled eggs, and stuffing were observed held beyond 7 days from the marked date of preparation. Food items were voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the deli slicer were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces of equipment are to be cleaned at a frequency necessary to preclude the buildup of food and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/11/2026

The following areas of the physical facilities were observed in need of adjustment/replacement: - The ceiling in the kitchen area is covered with tiles that are not smooth, durable, and easily cleanable - The wood shelving unit over the 3-bay sink area is in need of sealing Proper materials for kitchen surfaces were discussed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2026

The floor in the kitchen area was observed to have an accumulation of soil residue and debris under/behind equipment. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/18/2026

The following food items found cooling at the time of inspection were observed in need of adjustment to cooling methods... - Lasagna soup was observed to be cooling in a covered plastic container at 55 degrees Fahrenheit in the single door reach-in - Chicken was observed cooling densely piled in a metal pan in the single door cooler at 96 degrees Fahrenheit Food items prepared in the food establishment and set for cooling are to be arranged in the EQUIPMENT to provide maximum heat transfer through the container walls, spread out in thin layers, and loosely covered, or uncovered if protected from overhead contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 04/11/2024
